title: Olives: The Life and Lore of a Noble Fruit
description: A journalist's history and personal account of olives, olive oil and the people who grow them.

title: The Olive Farm
description: A memoir of buying and restoring a neglected olive farm on the French Riviera.

title: La vegetariana
description: A woman's decision to stop eating meat unravels her family in this Korean novel translated into Spanish.

title: Pachinko
description: A multigenerational saga following a Korean family making a life in twentieth-century Japan.

title: Extra Virginity: The Sublime and Scandalous World of Olive Oil
description: An investigation into the artistry, history and widespread adulteration of extra virgin olive oil.

title: The Land Where Lemons Grow
description: A history of Italy told through its citrus groves, growers and agrarian traditions.

title: The Olive Route
description: A journey around the Mediterranean tracing the cultural and agricultural history of the olive tree.

title: La clase de griego
description: A woman losing her speech and a man losing his sight meet in a Greek class, in this Korean novel.

title: Olivar Ecologico: Manual del Olivicultor
description: A practical handbook for managing certified organic olive groves in southern Spain.

title: Agricultura Ecologica y Calidad de los Alimentos
description: A study of how organic methods affect the nutritional and sensory quality of crops.

title: A Life of Ospreys
description: Decades of fieldwork bringing ospreys back to the skies of the Scottish Highlands.

title: Aves de Espana
description: A comprehensive field guide to the birds of the Iberian Peninsula.

title: The Omnivore's Dilemma
description: An examination of where our food comes from and the choices behind every meal.

title: Maangchi's Real Korean Cooking
description: Authentic Korean home recipes from banchan to fermented staples like kimchi.

title: Guia Penin de los Vinos de Espana
description: An annual reference rating Spanish wines, useful background for an estate export business.

title: South from Granada
description: Life in a remote Andalusian village in the years before the Spanish Civil War.

title: Driving Over Lemons
description: An Englishman's account of buying and farming a remote estate in the Alpujarras.

title: Kimchi y Aceite de Oliva
description: Essays and recipes pairing Korean fermentation with Mediterranean olive oil traditions.

title: Who Really Feeds the World?
description: An argument for small farms and biodiversity over industrial agriculture.

title: Casa de Misericordia
description: An award-winning poetry collection on memory, loss and shelter, in Catalan and Spanish.

title: Donde se Esconden los Pajaros
description: Reflective essays on the patience and pleasures of watching birds in the Spanish countryside.

title: How to Be an Urban Birder
description: A friendly guide to spotting birds anywhere, from city rooftops to country groves.
